description: Trigger-precision eval set: prompt -> which skill should hydrate (null = none). PASS semantics: give a router ONLY the 9 L1 descriptions; it must select one of the skills in `expected` (string = exactly one acceptable; array = any listed is a pass); should_trigger=false -> it must select NONE. Runner: skills/run_evals.py (LLM judge via claude -p). Judge is stochastic: expect +/-2 on positives across runs; negatives must always reject. Last full runs 2026-06-11 (26 cases): 26/26, 24/26, 24/26. 2026-06-12 (29 cases, after pf-trigger addition): 28/29, 28/29 - misses were positive judge variance; negatives 7/7, neg-stackless-pf additionally stress-tested 5/5. 2026-06-12 harness fix: judge now runs in an EMPTY cwd (agentic claude -p had been exploring the repo instead of answering -> picked=None) + one retry on unparseable positives; post-fix full run 29/29. 2026-06-12 boundary fix: custom-constraints made the single door for ALL system-wide caps (step 0 = native GlobalConstraint before linopy); cc-pos-1 re-tightened to single owner, 4/4 consistent + full 29/29. Pattern after Power-Agent/PowerSkills surge evals. 2026-06-13 (degradation depth, tasks-bess): +4 cases (ae-pos-deg, nm-pos-deg, pr-pos-deg [boundary: wear-range owned by asset-economics, physical-realism reconciles -> both accepted], deg-disambig). Added network-modeling 'StorageUnit vs Store+Links choice | degradation-aware storage representation' + asset-economics degradation-economics triggers; dropped bare 'wear cost' (collided with physical-realism plausibility). Routing-boundary spot-runs (new cases + nm/ae/pr positives + negatives) all green, nm-pos-deg stable 3/3 after the network-modeling anchor. LLM judge, internal regression check. Full 33-case run 2026-06-13 post-fix: 33/33 (all positives correct, negatives 7/7 reject).

cases.prompt: Add a 4-hour battery and an HVDC link to my model
Add an extendable transmission line - what capital_cost convention applies?
My optimization returns infeasible, what now?
The solve takes 6 hours, speed it up
Sanity-check my solved model
cases.note: README boundary prompt names solve-and-debug (battery); physical-realism is a defensible lexical match - both accepted

cases.prompt: Add a CO2 budget to my hydrogen model
cases.note: single door for system-wide caps; its step 0 = native GlobalConstraint before linopy

cases.prompt: Did linopy actually apply my reserve constraint?
Is this electrolyzer efficiency plausible?
Give me a pre-delivery checklist for my sector-coupled model
Can I merge Germany into one bus for this study?
Compute the congestion rent on the north-south corridor
Why do my model prices differ from EPEX?
What does this battery earn trading day-ahead and intraday?
Is the LCOH from my electrolyzer bankable?
How should I price battery degradation in my BESS revenue model?
Should a degradation-aware battery be a StorageUnit or Store+Links?
Is a 30 EUR/MWh battery wear cost plausible?
cases.note: boundary: the grid-BESS wear range/formula is OWNED by storage-revenue.md (asset-economics) and physical-realism reconciles to it (ranges-storage.md) -> either accepted; asset-economics is the owner of the number.

cases.prompt: How do I model battery degradation in PyPSA?
cases.note: ambiguous bare prompt; front-door rule: model/represent -> network-modeling, price/earn -> asset-economics. Both accepted, network-modeling preferred.

cases.prompt: Build wind capacity factors for Spain from ERA5
Update my 2020 gas price assumption to something realistic
Model district heating with heat pumps and a storage tank
Increase the cluster count in my PyPSA-Eur config and add offshore wind
Plot the dispatch for the worst week
Make my results presentable for the client
I ran n.optimize() on my nodal German grid model - will the dispatch actually hold up in an AC power flow?
Check AC feasibility of my solved PyPSA network with n.pf before I report loading numbers
Run an AC load flow on my pandapower case39 and check voltages
cases.note: pandapower stack - no pypsa-* skill may fire

cases.prompt: Open the PSS/E sav case and run a fault study with psspy
Run a transient stability time-domain simulation after the fault
Plot a histogram of my sales data in matplotlib
cases.note: generic plotting, no energy context - reporting must not fire

cases.prompt: Check breaker duty for the new substation
Hosting capacity for rooftop PV on this OpenDSS feeder
run a power flow and check voltages on case39
cases.note: stack unstated - hard case after n.pf triggers were added; must still reject
